[Sat 2026-07-04 23:07 PDT] [Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]

You are a SwarmLab builder agent. Build ONE experiment end-to-end: **09-overnight-cathedral**.

Working dir: ~/.openclaw/agents/scout/workspace/swarmlab

CONTEXT: This is a TypeScript (strict, ESM, Node 22+) monorepo. Experiments 01-08 are already built and committed — READ two or three of them first (e.g. experiments/04-consensus-under-lies/, experiments/08-rumor-mill/) to match the established pattern exactly: how they import from core/, how they run, how the trace is written/verified, how the README is structured. Read core/README.md and core/src/types.ts for the substrate contracts (TraceEvent, RunRecord, AgentSpec, spawn/bus/score/trace + StubRuntime).

EXPERIMENT 09 — The Overnight Cathedral: One spec, agents build iteratively over a long horizon; each commit/unit is reviewed by the next agent. Measure drift/quality over a long chain of iterative build steps under no human supervision. Faculty it informs: Lattice (governance of long-horizon unsupervised work) + Sonder (audit trail). Design a concrete, runnable simulation on core/ using StubRuntime for determinism, with an optional cheap real-LLM exhibition if trivial. It must produce a real, replayable trace and a fitness/observation score (e.g. quality decay, drift, review-catch rate over N iterations).

DELIVERABLES (match how 01-08 did each):
1. specs/09-overnight-cathedral.md — hypothesis, design, event shape, metrics, Definition of Done.
2. experiments/09-overnight-cathedral/ — built on core/, traceable/replayable/scored.
3. Typecheck passes (use the repo's existing tsc/build setup — check package.json).
4. Run it; verify a valid JSONL trace is produced and replay works.
5. experiments/09-overnight-cathedral/README.md — hypothesis + what you observed + which faculty it informs.
6. Append a note to JOURNAL.md (match existing entry …
